tr.td: 1 tablet (40 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product
Live attenuated Human alphaherpesvirus 3 only vaccine product

ul.li: Patient will monitor and record weight daily to detect early signs of fluid retention.
Patient will limit daily sodium intake as recommended to reduce fluid overload.
Patient will restrict daily fluid intake as advised to prevent worsening heart failure.
Patient will monitor and record blood pressure regularly to manage heart failure and hypertension.
Patient will take prescribed heart failure medications as directed without missed doses.
Patient will recognize and promptly report symptoms such as increased shortness of breath, swelling, or rapid weight gain.
Increase patient understanding of hypertension and promote lifestyle modifications to support blood pressure control

tr.td: Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported
Blood pressure panel with all children optional
1 tablet (40 mg) by mouth once daily

text.value: 1 tablet (40 mg) by mouth once daily

tr.td: 1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily

text.value: 1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily

tr.td: Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product

display.value: Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
influenza, inactivated, split virus or surface antigen

text.value: Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product

tr.td: Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product

display.value: Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product
tetanus toxoid, combinations with diphtheria toxoid

text.value: Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product

tr.td: Live attenuated Human alphaherpesvirus 3 only vaccine product

ul.li: Patient will monitor and record weight daily to detect early signs of fluid retention.
Patient will limit daily sodium intake as recommended to reduce fluid overload.
Patient will restrict daily fluid intake as advised to prevent worsening heart failure.
Patient will monitor and record blood pressure regularly to manage heart failure and hypertension.
Patient will take prescribed heart failure medications as directed without missed doses.
Patient will recognize and promptly report symptoms such as increased shortness of breath, swelling, or rapid weight gain.

display.value: Heart failure self management plan (record artifact)

ul.li: Increase patient understanding of hypertension and promote lifestyle modifications to support blood pressure control
